name: Sullivan's No Wandering Domain Theorem
description: Sullivan's Theorem (1985): For a rational map f: ℂ̂→ℂ̂ of degree ≥ 2, every Fatou component is eventually periodic. Equivalently: rational maps have no wandering domains. Proof: assume wandering U exists; Baker's lemma ⇒ U eventually simply-connected; deform conformal structure along grand orbit via Beltrami differentials; MRMT yields infinite-dimensional family of conjugate rational maps; contradiction (Rat_d is finite-dimensional).
mermaid:
graph TD
    Julia["Julia & Fatou sets"]
    MRMT["Measurable Riemann Mapping"]
    DefWander["Def: Wandering domain\nIterates disjoint"]
    DefPeriodic["Def: Eventually periodic\nf^n(U)=f^m(U)"]
    LemBaker["Lem: Baker\nWandering ⇒ eventually simply-connected"]
    ThmNWD["Thm: Sullivan\nNo wandering domains"]
    LemDeform["Lem: QC deformation\nBeltrami along grand orbit"]
    LemFinDim["Lem: Rat_d finite-dimensional\nZariski open in ℂP^{2d+1}"]
